article_title: Liquid AI's 230M-Parameter LFM2.5 Beats Models Four Times Its Size at Data Extraction and Runs on a Raspberry Pi
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: Liquid AI's 230M-Parameter LFM2.5 Beats Models Four Times Its Size at Data Extraction and Runs on a Raspberry Pi
content_preview.summary: The MIT spinout's smallest model yet pairs convolution and attention blocks to outscore billion-parameter rivals on data extraction while decoding at 42 tokens per second on a Raspberry Pi 5.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Liquid AI has released LFM2.5-230M, a 230-million-parameter foundation model that the company says outperforms substantially larger models on data extraction tasks while running on hardware as constrained as a Raspberry Pi. According to [Liquid AI](https://www.liquid.ai/blog/lfm2-5-230m), the model is built to "run anywhere," from cloud GPUs to low-cost CPUs, and ships as open weights that developers can "Download, fine-tune, and deploy without restrictions."

The model is the small...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Clean, well-structured News piece (506 words, within the 400-1200 range). Strong Overview/What We Know/What We Don't Know/Analysis scaffolding. The 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately caveats that benchmark figures are vendor-reported and not independently reproduced.
editor_notes.source_verification: All three snapshots read from disk and verified (manifest 200 on all). source-0.html.gz (Liquid AI blog): confirms 'Run Anywhere', 'Download, fine-tune, and deploy without restrictions' verbatim, CaseReportBench 22.51 / Qwen3.5-0.8B 13.83 / Gemma 3 1B 2.28, IFEval 71.71/59.94/63.49, GPQA Diamond 25.41, '213 tok/s'/'42 tok/s', 'surprisingly capable at tool use and data extraction tasks' verbatim, and 'pre-trained for 19T tokens'. source-1.html.gz (MarkTechPost): confirms 'Eight are double-gated LIV convolution blocks' and 'The remaining six are grouped-query attention (GQA) blocks' verbatim, 32,768 context, 65,536 vocab, '293-375 MB', knowledge cutoff mid-2024, license lfm1.0, runtime list 'llama.cpp, MLX, vLLM, SGLang, and ONNX', and 'pre-trained on 19 trillion tokens'. source-2.html.gz (Liquid about page): confirms 'Spun out of MIT CSAIL' and the four founders verbatim — Ramin Hasani (CEO), Mathias Lechner (CTO), Alexander Amini (CSO), Daniela Rus.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: One factual error found: the article states the model uses a 'hybrid 16-block layout' but MarkTechPost says 'The model has 14 layers total' (8 LIV convolution + 6 GQA = 14, not 16). The article's own quoted figures (eight + six) contradict its '16-block' framing. This is a single recoverable numeric error covered by the corrections record. Separately, the company valuation/funding figures ($2.35B Series A) that circulated only in search snippets were correctly OMITTED (absent from all three sources, not fabricated); VentureBeat (which bot-blocked) is not cited. All benchmark/performance claims are vendor-reported and properly attributed to Liquid AI, not presented as independent fact.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and measured. The headline's 'Beats Models Four Times Its Size' is framed in-body as a company benchmark claim (CaseReportBench: 230M vs Gemma 3 1B at ~4.3x params), with explicit vendor-reported caveats. No sensationalism or editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No prior Liquid AI or LFM coverage in the archive.
concerns: Article says 'hybrid 16-block layout'; MarkTechPost says the model has 14 layers total (8 LIV convolution + 6 GQA). Numeric error filed as a correction.
Minor: the quoted phrase 'target fast CPU inference' alters the source's verb tense ('targets fast CPU inference'). Trivial, not corrections-worthy on its own.
recommendations: Allowlist warning is NON-BLOCKING: liquid.ai (the first-party primary source) is not on config/source_allowlist.txt. A maintainer may consider adding liquid.ai as a trusted first-party domain. Allowlist file left unedited.

With only 3 sources (2 first-party), the 'beats 4x larger models' claim should always be read as a company benchmark assertion; the article handles this correctly via the 'What We Don't Know' caveats.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively accurate, well-sourced, neutral News piece. A single numeric error (16-block vs 14-layer) is recoverable via a public corrections record; the headline, summary, and lead do not depend on it. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
